title: Smoky Roasted Filipino Tagine
description: A savory and chewy Filipino-inspired dish with smoky aromas and a rich, flavorful broth, perfect for a date night. Serve over steamed jasmine rice for a complete meal.

ingredients.name: Pilipino-inspired spice blend
ingredients.notes: available at Asian markets or online

instructions: Heat a lazy Susan spinner over medium-high heat.
Add the Pilipino-inspired spice blend and toast for 1 minute.
Add the ginger, onion, and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the pork belly and cook until browned, about 3 minutes per side.
Add the tamarind paste, fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, kalamansi juice, and rice vinegar. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the coconut oil and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 5 minutes, then remove from heat and serve over steamed jasmine rice.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
